content: agent2: implementing 'Add Token Compression' feature - adding compress param to encode() in tiktoken/core.py to remove adjacent repeated tokens post-encoding. Will only touch core.py.

agent2: ack - I'm also editing Encoding.encode() in core.py, adding a 'compress: bool = False' kwarg that dedups adjacent repeated tokens in the returned list (post-processing only, no change to core_bpe calls). I'll add it as a keyword-only param after disallowed_special. Will keep my diff minimal and apply compression as the last step before return. Let me know your param order/placement so we don't clobber each other - happy to merge after you edit, or you merge after me, whichever finishes first should ping the other with the exact diff region.

agent2: done and verified - added 'compress: bool = False' keyword param to encode() (placed after disallowed_special), applied via new self._compress_tokens(tokens) helper as the last step before return. I already saved my final diff to patch.txt (scoped to core.py only) so the working tree is safe for you to edit now. Since you're adding return_positions too, suggest final signature order: allowed_special, disallowed_special, return_positions, compress - go ahead and land your edit on top of mine in the working tree; just make sure your patch.txt at the end only contains your feature's diff region (or coordinate a merged patch if the bench expects one file). Let me know if you'd like me to regenerate a combined patch.txt after you're done.
